prompt: Casual 'save it as a learning insight' with no slash command

assertions.description: A .md file was created in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ — this confirms the skill triggered without an explicit /capture-insight slash command
The created file contains all four required sections: ## The Struggle, ## The Drill-Down, ## The Aha Moment, ## The One-Liner
The file content references the '28%' RAGAS score improvement — a specific detail from the prompt that should be preserved, not paraphrased away

The Struggle and Drill-Down sections use the user's own phrasing and energy. User said 'barely moved the needle' and 'spent two days' — the card should preserve this first-person, practical tone, not elevate it into formal writing

The Aha Moment correctly captures the insight that chunk boundaries (not embedding model size) were the root cause of retrieval failures. The mental model shift — from 'model quality' to 'chunk quality' — should be clear
